findings.description: The PDF uses a structured time-based layout with times in a left column and content in a right column, resembling a two-column schedule format. The HTML converts this into a flat bulleted list, losing the tabular time-content pairing.
findings.evidence: PDF shows times (9:00 a.m., 9:10 a.m., 9:30 a.m., 10:00 a.m.) aligned left with corresponding content indented to the right in a schedule format. HTML renders these as bullet points with times bolded inline.

findings.description: The PDF title 'AGENDA' is displayed in a significantly larger, bold font as a prominent heading. The HTML renders it as a smaller section heading.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'AGENDA' in very large bold font, centered. HTML: 'AGENDA' as a smaller heading.

findings.description: The centered formatting of the title block (Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting, AGENDA, Teleconference via WebEx, May 14, 2020) is changed to left-aligned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: Title block is centered. HTML: Title and subtitle are left-aligned.

findings.description: Page 2 of the PDF uses the same time-based schedule layout with times on the left and content on the right. The HTML converts this to a flat bulleted list structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows times (11:15 a.m., 12:15 p.m., 1:15 p.m., 2:30 p.m., 3:00 p.m., 3:30 p.m., 3:45 p.m., 4:00 p.m.) with corresponding content. HTML uses bullet lists.

findings.description: The footer notes about times and probable action items appear at the bottom of each PDF page but are repositioned in the HTML - appearing as a bullet point at the end and also at the top of the continuation page.
findings.evidence: PDF: Footer at bottom of each page. HTML: Note appears as inline bullet text at both the top and bottom of content sections.

findings.description: The heading hierarchy uses h1 for the document title, then h2 for 'AGENDA', then h3 for both 'Teleconference via WebEx' and 'May 14, 2020'. The date and location are not section headings in the PDF — they are subtitle/subheading text under the title. Using h3 for these is questionable but minor. More importantly, 'AGENDA' as h2 is acceptable since it is a prominent subtitle in the PDF.
findings.evidence:
HTML:

AGENDA

,

Teleconference via WebEx

,

May 14, 2020

. In PDF these are centered subtitle text, not structural section headings.

findings.description: The page header/footer text is duplicated in the HTML. The footer note '* Times indicated are merely for guidance...' and '**Probable Action Items' appears three times in the HTML (once at top, once mid-document as page 2 header/footer, and once at the very end), whereas in the PDF it only appears as repeated page footers.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '* Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commission will proceed through the agenda until completed. **Probable Action Items 1' at the top, '**Probable Action Items 2' mid-document, and again '**Probable Action Items 2' at the very end. The page header 'Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda' also appears as plain text twice in the body.

findings.description: The agenda items are rendered as an unstructured bullet list rather than reflecting the time-based structure of the PDF agenda. While this is a formatting/structural choice, it flattens the visual hierarchy of the document somewhat.
findings.evidence: PDF uses a time-based left-column layout (9:00 a.m., 9:10 a.m., etc.) with indented sub-items. HTML uses a flat unordered list with bold times.
